prompt:
You are shown one page from a SEC 10-K filing rendered as an image.
Item "10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" ENDS on this page; the next section is "Item 11. Executive Compensation".
First locate the bottom edge of Item 10's text:
- If the "Item 11" heading is visible on this page, Item 10 ends on the line directly ABOVE that heading.
- Otherwise Item 10 runs all the way to the very bottom of the page.
Then transcribe VERBATIM the LAST 5 lines of Item 10 at that bottom edge — the FINAL lines right before the section ends, NOT lines from the top or middle of the page. Ignore running headers, page numbers, footers.
Output ONLY the transcribed text, no commentary, no quotes, no formatting.
text:
The image provided is a blank page with only the page number "57" at the top. There is no visible text from Item 10, Item 11 heading, or any other content on this page. Since Item 10 is stated to END on this page but the page is entirely blank, and there is no Item 11 heading visible, this appears to be a page where Item 10 has already ended on a previous page, or this is a divider page.

However, following the instructions: if Item 10 ends on this page and there's no Item 11 heading visible, then technically Item 10 would run to the bottom—but there is no text at all on this page.

Given the page is completely blank with no text content to transcribe, the last 5 lines of Item 10 on this page would be empty. But since I need to output something and the instructions say to transcribe verbatim the last 5 lines, and there are no lines of text on this page, I must indicate that there is no text.

Since the page is entirely blank with no visible text content, there are no lines to transcribe. The page contains only the page number "57" which should be ignored per instructions.

(No text exists on the provided page image to transcribe.)
